questions.question: Explain the meaning of drainage.
questions.answer: Drainage refers to the river system of an area. It includes rivers, streams and tributaries that carry water and drain it into a larger water body such as a sea or ocean.
answer_outline: Water flowing to seas or oceans

questions.question: Explain the concept of a drainage basin.
questions.answer: A drainage basin is the area drained by a single river and its tributaries. All the water in this region flows towards the same river system.

questions.question: Explain what a water divide is.
questions.answer: A water divide is a highland or upland that separates two drainage basins and determines the direction of river flow.

questions.question: Explain the two major river systems of India.
questions.answer: India has two major river systems: Himalayan rivers and Peninsular rivers. Himalayan rivers originate from glaciers and are perennial, while Peninsular rivers originate from plateaus and depend mainly on rainfall.

questions.question: Explain the characteristics of Himalayan rivers.
questions.answer: Himalayan rivers originate from glaciers, flow throughout the year and carry large amounts of water and silt. They form deep valleys and fertile plains.

questions.question: Explain the Indus river system.
questions.answer: The Indus river originates near Lake Mansarowar in Tibet. It flows through Ladakh and is joined by tributaries such as the Zaskar, Shyok and Nubra before entering Pakistan.

questions.question: Explain the Ganga river system.
questions.answer: The Ganga originates from the Gangotri glacier. It is joined by many tributaries such as Yamuna, Ghaghara, Gandak and Kosi before flowing into the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Explain the Brahmaputra river system.
questions.answer: The Brahmaputra originates in Tibet as the Tsang Po and enters India through Arunachal Pradesh. It flows through Assam and forms many river islands.

questions.question: Explain the characteristics of Peninsular rivers.
questions.answer: Peninsular rivers originate from the Peninsular plateau and depend mainly on rainfall. They have shorter courses and smaller drainage basins.

questions.question: Explain the Narmada river basin.
questions.answer: The Narmada river originates in the Amarkantak hills and flows westwards through a rift valley before joining the Arabian Sea.

questions.question: Explain the Godavari river basin.
questions.answer: The Godavari river originates in Maharashtra and flows eastwards into the Bay of Bengal. It has the largest drainage basin among peninsular rivers.

questions.question: Explain the Krishna river basin.
questions.answer: The Krishna river originates near Mahabaleshwar and flows eastwards into the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Explain the Kaveri river basin.
questions.answer: The Kaveri originates in the Western Ghats and flows through Karnataka and Tamil Nadu before entering the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Explain the importance of lakes.
questions.answer: Lakes regulate river flow, provide water for irrigation and drinking, generate hydel power and support tourism.

questions.question: Explain the types of lakes found in India.
questions.answer: Lakes in India can be freshwater or saltwater lakes. Some are natural while others are formed by human activities like dams.

questions.question: Explain the role of rivers in the economy.
questions.answer: Rivers provide water for agriculture, support transportation, generate hydroelectric power and support settlements.

questions.question: Explain how rivers support agriculture.
questions.answer: Rivers supply water for irrigation and deposit fertile alluvial soil that supports crop production.

questions.question: Explain how rivers help in transportation.
questions.answer: Rivers act as natural waterways that help transport goods and people.

questions.question: Explain the causes of river pollution.
questions.answer: River pollution is caused by untreated sewage, industrial waste and agricultural chemicals.

questions.question: Explain the effects of river pollution.
questions.answer: River pollution harms aquatic life, contaminates drinking water and affects human health.

questions.question: Explain the need for river conservation.
questions.answer: River conservation is necessary to maintain water quality, protect ecosystems and ensure sustainable use of water resources.

questions.question: Explain the Ganga Action Plan.
questions.answer: The Ganga Action Plan was launched to clean the Ganga river and reduce pollution levels.

questions.question: Explain the importance of clean rivers.
questions.answer: Clean rivers provide safe drinking water, support ecosystems and maintain environmental balance.

questions.question: Explain why rivers are important natural resources.
questions.answer: Rivers provide water, fertile soil and energy resources essential for human survival.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between rivers and human settlements.
questions.answer: Human settlements often develop near rivers because they provide water, fertile land and transportation routes.
